---
title: "Shiny for Submission"
subtitle: "Updates from the R Consortium R Submissions Working Group"
author: PHUSE US Connect 2026 · AV09 {teal} Advance Session \

  Sam Parmar
date: "2026-03-25"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    smaller: true
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    transition: slide
    logo: ""
    footer: "R Consortium R Submissions WG · PHUSE US Connect 2026 · [parmsam.github.io/2026-phuse-us-connect-teal-adv](https://parmsam.github.io/2026-phuse-us-connect-teal-adv)"
    code-overflow: wrap
    highlight-style: github

resources:
  - assets/
---

## The Journey So Far {.center footer=false}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Completed Pilots**

| Pilot | Focus | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| 1 | TLFs via pkglite | ✅ FDA accepted |
| 2 | Shiny app submission | ✅ FDA accepted |
| 3 | ADaM datasets in R | ✅ FDA accepted Aug 2024 |
| 4 | WebAssembly + Containers | ✅ Submitted 2024–2025 |
| 5 | Dataset-JSON | 🔄 Resubmitted Jan 2026, under review |
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Upcoming**

| Pilot | Focus |
|-------|-------|
| 6 | Expanded ADaMs + displays |
| 7 | Simulated CDISC data |

::: {.callout-note}
All materials are **publicly available** on [GitHub](https://github.com/RConsortium)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Set context — this WG has been systematically proving R is viable for FDA submissions. We're focusing today on the Shiny-specific story: Pilots 2 and 4.
:::

---

## Pilot 2: The First Shiny App to FDA {.center .smaller footer=false}

**Goal:** Example R-based submission containing a Shiny app, submitted through the current FDA e-submission system and process

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**What was submitted**

- Shiny app displaying the 4 TLFs from Pilot 1
- Basic dataset filtering functionalities
- Shiny code packaged as a **proprietary R package** via `{pkglite}`
- Submitted via the eCTD portal (~2022 H1); **accepted December 2022**
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**What FDA had to do**

- Deploy the Shiny app on FDA infrastructure (laptop/server)
- Reproduce the analysis results
- Requested removal of p-values from filtered tables (p-hacking risk)
:::
:::


**Team:** Roche · Atorus · Merck · Eli Lilly · **FDA:** Paul Schuette · Hye Soo Cho · **Reviewed:** Oct 2023

::: {.callout-important}
This was the **first time** a Shiny application was submitted and accepted through the FDA eCTD gateway.
:::

::: {.notes}
Key point: the FDA had to actually install and run the app themselves on their own infrastructure. This was a significant logistical hurdle — hence the motivation for Pilot 4.
:::

## {#pilot2-demo data-menu-title="Pilot 2 Demo Video"}

{{<video "assets/video/pilot2_app_demo_for_dia_compressed.mp4" >}}

::: footer
[github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot2](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot2)
:::

---

## Pilot 2 → Pilot 4: The Problem

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Pilot 2 limitation**

FDA reviewers needed to:

1. Set up an R environment
2. Install all package dependencies
3. Run the Shiny server locally

This creates friction in the review process and reproducibility risk.

**Pilot 4 question:**

> *Can we ship the entire execution environment with the app?*
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
**Two approaches explored:**

```
Pilot 4
├── 🐳 Containers
│   └── Docker runtime
│   └── Full system + R deps
│
└── 🌐 WebAssembly
    └── Runs in browser
    └── No R install needed
    └── via {webR}
```
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
This is the logical evolution — Pilot 2 proved the concept, Pilot 4 tried to make it practical.
:::

---

## Pilot 4: WebAssembly Track

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
**What is WebAssembly?**

Compiles applications into a portable, self-contained bundle that can be run directly within a web browser.

**Key tools:**
- `{webR}` — R compiled to WebAssembly
- `{httpuv}` — embedded web server
- Pilot 2 Shiny app adapted for webR constraints

**Submission milestone:**
WebAssembly package reached FDA eCTD gateway on **September 20, 2024**
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-tip}
## FDA Reviewer Experience
1. Extract submission bundle
2. Run setup code to start local web server
3. Open app in Microsoft Edge

No Shiny server. No R environment needed. Runs in the browser.
:::

**Repo:** [submissions-pilot4-webR](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot4-webR)

**Team:** Eli Lilly · Pfizer · Appsilon · J&J · Biogen · BMS · GSK
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
WebR is a genuinely remarkable piece of technology — R running entirely in the browser. The Pilot 2 app had to be adapted but most functionality was preserved.
:::

## {#pilot4demo data-menu-title="Pilot 4 WebAssembly Demo Video"}

{{<video "assets/video/pilot4_webassembly_demo.mp4" aspect-ratio="16x9">}}

::: footer
[submissions-pilot4-webr.netlify.app](https://submissions-pilot4-webr.netlify.app/)
:::

---

## Pilot 4: Container Track

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Docker container approach**

- Full Linux environment bundled
- R + all package dependencies included
- Submitted Summer 2025, tested across ~15 FDA environments
- Docker issues encountered were resolvable

::: {.callout-important}
## Critical finding (Dec 2025)
**WSL is no longer permitted on FDA reviewer computers** due to a security policy update. Docker on Windows requires WSL — making Docker-based submissions effectively blocked at FDA.
:::
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.callout-warning}
## Unexpected finding
FDA reviewers **preferred WebAssembly** over containers — contrary to the team's expectation.

WebAssembly only needed a browser. No WSL, no IT approval, no firewall issues.
:::

**Repos:** [submissions-pilot4-container](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot4-container) · [to-fda](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot4-container-to-fda)
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
The WSL security policy change is a fundamental blocker — not just an inconvenience. Docker requires WSL on Windows, and FDA reviewers work on Windows. This emerged from testing across 15 environments in late 2025. The Pilot 4 summary report documents all findings and was nearing completion as of Feb 2026.
:::

---

## Key Outcomes

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Technical wins**

- Interactive Shiny apps viable in FDA submissions ✅
- WebAssembly: runs in-browser, no R install required ✅
- Containers: reproducible but **WSL ban blocks Docker on Windows** ⚠️
- FDA eCTD now accepts `.zip`, `.rds`, `.rdb`, `.rdx`, `.rda`, `.md`, `.rd` natively (Aug 2025)

> `{pkglite}` remains a valid, peer-reviewed approach — `.zip` is an added *option*, not a replacement
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Regulatory wins**

- FDA Pilot 3 response letter: R ADaMs fully accepted
- FDA verbal → written feedback loop established
- All pilots **publicly available** as templates

::: {.callout-note}
"Industry experts and FDA reviewers work collaboratively to improve regulatory science infrastructure"
:::
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
The eCTD format expansion is huge — it means you no longer need to use pkglite workarounds to submit R packages.
:::

---

## What This Means for {teal}

**The submission pathway is open**

Pilots 2 & 4 prove that Shiny apps **built on frameworks like {teal}** can be submitted to regulators.

**Key considerations for {teal} apps:**

- Package as proprietary R package (`{pkglite}` or `.zip`)
- Prefer CRAN-hosted packages — FDA reviewers favour known, vetted dependencies
- Document all dependencies in ADRG (more detail than SAS requires)
- Design for deployment on reviewer's Windows infrastructure
- For interactive filtering: avoid showing inferred statistics (p-values) on filtered subsets
- Consider WebAssembly constraints for Pilot 4-style delivery

::: {.notes}
Bring it back to the teal audience — these pilots are the proof of concept, teal is the framework that makes it practical at scale.
:::

---

## Discussion

**Open questions**

1. How do we handle `{teal}` module dependency validation?
2. WebAssembly for `{teal}` — what's feasible today? (Note: `{golem}` was incompatible with webR; framework choices matter)
3. Could Nix/Rix offer a lighter-weight alternative to containers for reproducible environments?

::: {.callout-tip}
## Workshop prompt
Have you tried deploying a {teal} app through your regulatory review process? What challenges did you face?
:::

---

## Resources & Next Steps {.center}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**R Submissions Working Group**

- Website: [rconsortium.github.io/submissions-wg](https://rconsortium.github.io/submissions-wg)
- GitHub: [github.com/RConsortium](https://github.com/RConsortium)
- Contact: director@r-consortium.org

**Key repositories**
- [submissions-pilot2](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot2)
- [submissions-pilot4-webR](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot4-webR)
- [submissions-pilot4-container](https://github.com/RConsortium/submissions-pilot4-container)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Get involved**

- Join the WG — open to all
- Pilots 6 & 7 launching 2026
- Pilot 6: expanded ADaMs + AI/automation (won't be submitted to FDA)
- Pilot 7: realistic simulated CDISC data
- Future: Nix/Rix for reproducible environments; AI-assisted ADRG generation under discussion

:::
:::

**References**
- [2026 WG Update](https://r-consortium.org/posts/submissions-wg-2026/)
- [Pilot 2 overview](https://rconsortium.github.io/submissions-wg/pilot2.html)
- [Pilot 4 overview](https://rconsortium.github.io/submissions-wg/pilot4.html)
- [FDA eCTD format expansion](https://r-consortium.org/posts/expanded-fda-ectd-file-format-support-for-r-packages/)
- [PharmaSUG 2024 SS-344](https://www.lexjansen.com/pharmasug/2024/SS/PharmaSUG-2024-SS-344.pdf)
- [Appsilon: R in FDA Submissions — 5 Pilots](https://www.appsilon.com/post/r-in-fda-submissions)
- [Eric Nantz (NESS 2025): A Whole New (Open) World](https://rpodcast.github.io/opensub-ness2025/)
